title: Perplexity API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Perplexity API key detected in source code with pplx- prefix. Perplexity API keys provide access to AI-powered search, answer generation, and language model capabilities. Exposing these keys allows unauthorized access to consume your API quota, incur unexpected charges, access search results and AI responses, and potentially abuse the service for malicious queries. Store keys securely using environment variables or secret management systems. Revoke exposed keys immediately from the Perplexity dashboard.

Perplexity API key detected with sk-pplx- prefix format. This key grants access to Perplexity's AI search and language model services. Unauthorized access can lead to quota exhaustion, billing fraud, and service abuse. IMMEDIATELY revoke this key from your Perplexity account dashboard and rotate credentials. Use environment variables (PERPLEXITY_API_KEY) or secure secret management systems (AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, HashiCorp Vault) for storage.
